The spring basin is 50 feet deep at the mouth of the cavern. Surface lit during the day and inhabited by Koi, Gar, Catfish, American Eels, Blue Gill, and Bass to name a few.  The cavern continues another one hundred yards, to a depth of 115 feet.
Jackson Blue is one of the cave systems feeding the Merritt Mill Pond near Marianna, FL. It has a beautiful cavern zone, and the cave system is is beautiful, with clean white limestone.
Boasting crystal-clear water that provides virtually perfect visibility, the 250-foot main pool is one of the area’s most unique diving expeditions.
Dive into the captivating world of Devil's Den Prehistoric Spring, one of Florida's premier scuba diving destinations. This underground spring is hidden within a 120-foot wide cave.

Lauderdale-by-the-Sea is nicknamed the “Shore Dive Capital of South Florida” for a reason: its living coral reefs are a mere 100–400 feet from the beach, making it one of the easiest and most accessible dive spots in the world.

Molasses Reef is a world-renowned, easily accessible coral reef located southeast of Key Largo in the Florida Keys National Marine Sanctuary, famous for its stunning clear water, diverse coral formations

Saint Andrews Jetties at St. Andrews State Park near Panama City Beach, Florida, is a wonderful dive site. There are picnic tables available to assemble scuba kits and plan dives. The water is accessible from shore.
